# LAF Enterprise ERP vs ERPNext — Honest Parity Comparison

**Date:** 2026-06-21
**Product:** LAF Enterprise ERP (clean-room, generated by/with LocalAIFactory)
**Machine-readable scores:** `benchmarks/erpnext-study/laf-erp-vs-erpnext-score.json`
**License/clean-room basis:** `docs/reports/ERP_CLONE_LICENSE_AND_CLEAN_ROOM_DECISION.md`

> **This is not an ERPNext clone and not ERPNext-compatible.** It is an original C#/.NET ERP built
> clean-room from ERPNext/Frappe **official documentation** and public domain/accounting theory. No
> source code or UI assets were copied. ERPNext is GPLv3 and Frappe core is MIT; since nothing
> copyleft was copied, copyleft is not triggered (see the license decision report).

## Headline scores (honest self-assessment)

| Measure | Score |
|---|---:|
| Functional parity | **32%** |
| Technical quality | 78% |
| Workflow / control parity | 65% |
| API parity | 45% |
| UI parity | 30% |
| Report parity | 30% |
| Security / control parity | 42% |
| Test confidence | 75% |
| Generated-ERP production readiness | 35% |
| **Overall ERPNext-grade score** | **≈ 36%** |

ERPNext is a very large, mature platform (15+ modules, hundreds of doctypes and reports). This is a
**single-sprint clean-room core**. The score reflects that honestly: a real, tested double-entry +
inventory + maker/checker spine across ~9 modules, with several modules absent.

## Category-by-category

| Category | Parity | Score | Evidence / gap |
|---|---|---:|---|
| Core setup | StrongPartial | 68 | Company/FY/Currency/CoA/CostCenter/Numbering/Tax/multi-co/multi-wh |
| Accounting | Partial | 55 | GL/JE/Payment/AR/AP/Trial Balance ✅; no P&L/BS/aging/period-close |
| Inventory | Partial | 48 | Stock ledger + moving-avg valuation + negative guard ✅; no stock-entry/FIFO/batch |
| Selling | Partial | 42 | Customer/SO/Sales Invoice + workflow ✅; no quotation/delivery/returns |
| Purchasing | Partial | 42 | Supplier/PO/Purchase Invoice + workflow ✅; no receipt/RFQ/returns |
| CRM | Skeleton | 35 | Lead/Opportunity/convert |
| Projects | Skeleton | 30 | Project/Task + approval gating |
| Support | Skeleton | 30 | Ticket/escalate/resolve |
| Assets | Skeleton | 25 | Asset + maintenance flag; no depreciation |
| Manufacturing | **Missing** | 0 | — |
| HR / Payroll | **Missing** | 0 | — |
| Website / eCommerce | **Missing** | 0 | — |
| POS | **Missing** | 0 | — |
| Reports | Partial | 30 | GL/Trial Balance/Stock Balance/AR-AP; no report builder |
| Workflows | StrongPartial | 65 | maker/checker + threshold + reject-reason + immutability + reversal |
| Roles / permissions | StrongPartial | 60 | RolePermission matrix enforced |
| Audit trail | StrongPartial | 68 | AuditEvent on every action |
| APIs | Partial | 45 | REST core + reports + workflow/audit; not auto-per-doctype |
| Import / export | Skeleton | 25 | CSV customers with per-row errors |
| Customization | Skeleton | 18 | Numbering series; no custom-field UI |
| Deployment | Partial | 40 | SQLite zero-dep or SQL Server; EnsureCreated (no migrations) |
| Tests | StrongPartial | 72 | 74 xUnit + 12 Playwright, all passing |
| Security / controls | Partial | 42 | RBAC + maker/checker; dev-auth only |
| UI | Partial | 30 | 9 read pages + login; no create/edit forms yet |
| Documentation | StrongPartial | 65 | full design/reference/UAT/demo docs |

## What is genuinely strong

- **Double-entry general ledger** that refuses to post an unbalanced voucher and always balances
  globally (proved by tests).
- **Immutable stock ledger** with moving-average valuation and a negative-stock guard.
- **Maker/checker** with submitter-cannot-approve-own, amount-threshold approval, mandatory rejection
  reason, immutable posted documents, and reversal-not-edit on cancel — all test-proven.
- **Audit trail** on every state change.
- **86 automated tests** (74 .NET + 12 Playwright) all green; the app runs on SQLite with zero
  external services.

## What is missing or thin (no hiding)

Manufacturing, HR/Payroll, POS, and Website/eCommerce are **entirely absent**. Selling/Buying lack
quotation/delivery/receipt/returns. Reporting lacks P&L/Balance Sheet/aging. The UI is read-oriented
(records are created through the API/services, not UI forms). Authentication is a **dev cookie**, not
real auth. There are no EF migrations (schema via `EnsureCreated`).

## Bottom line

A credible **POC-grade** demonstration that LocalAIFactory can study a large open-source business
system from official docs and generate a real, tested, controls-aware ERP core — at roughly **36%**
overall ERPNext-grade. It is **not** a finished ERPNext alternative and makes no compatibility claim.
